title: PipelineConfig
description: Configuration schema for a design pipeline run. Controls elicitation mode, working directory, input manifest, agent access, and output options.

project_name.description: Human-readable project name. Used for namespacing output files and run-history entries.

working_dir.description: Absolute path to the pipeline working directory. All agent file reads and writes MUST be scoped to this directory. No agent may access paths outside this directory without explicit codebase_access permission.

pipeline_memory_dir.description: Absolute path to the shared pipeline memory directory. Only reflection-agent writes here; all other agents may read pattern files. Defaults to {working_dir}/../pipeline-memory/

elicitation_mode.description:
Controls how the pipeline gathers requirements from the user.
  FAST: Figma URL provided; 3 targeted questions; skips full requirements interview.
  SCAN: PRD or brief provided; gap-fill questions only.
  FULL: Vague or no input; 3 elicitation phases with review gates.
  DELTA: Prior run exists in run-history.json; only scope-change questions asked.

figma_url.description: Figma file URL. Required for FAST and DELTA modes, and for all runs that use the Figma-first pipeline path.

figma_page.description: Name of the Figma page to target. If omitted, the agent uses the current page.

prior_run_id.description: Run ID from run-history.json to use as baseline for DELTA mode. Required when elicitation_mode is DELTA.

pipeline_path.description:
Which pipeline path to execute.
  figma_first: Start from existing Figma file (requires figma_url).
  brief_first: Start from requirements/brief (no Figma file yet).
  auto: Detect based on whether figma_url is provided.

checkpoints.description: Mid-pipeline user review gates. All enabled by default.

A.description: Checkpoint A: Post-Stage 1 (requirements). Enabled when elicitation_mode is FULL or SCAN. User reviews inferred requirements before proceeding.

B.description: Checkpoint B: Post-Stage 4 (token mapping + component manifest). Always enabled. Last gate before Figma execution. User reviews layout/component plan.

C.description: Checkpoint C: Post-first-screen Figma execution. Always enabled. User confirms visual direction before remaining screens are built.

codebase_access.description: If true, delivery-sequencer may read the user's codebase to produce code-connect mappings and component import paths. Disabled by default.

codebase_root.description: Absolute path to the codebase root. Required when codebase_access is true.

codebase_include_patterns.description: Glob patterns for files the delivery-sequencer may read from the codebase (e.g., ['src/components/**/*.tsx']).

figma_write_agents.description: List of agent names explicitly permitted to execute write operations on the Figma file. Agents not in this list must treat the Figma file as read-only.

token_formats.description: Which token export formats to generate.

platform.description: Target platforms for motion specs and layout dimensions.

include_motion_spec.description: If true, motion-spec-agent runs and produces motion-spec.json.

include_brand_compliance.description: If true, brand-compliance-agent runs after delivery-sequencer.

creativity_mode.description: Controls how much creative latitude generative agents apply. 'structured' = honour brief constraints, one definitive output; 'balanced' = brief-first with creative judgment on open decisions; 'exploratory' = produce divergent alternatives for key decisions.

stage_overrides.description: Per-stage creativity overrides. Key is agent name (e.g. 'creative-director'), value is one of 'structured' | 'balanced' | 'exploratory'. Overrides the top-level creativity_mode for that stage.

enabled.description: Whether this stage runs in the pipeline.

max_retries.description: Maximum number of correction loop iterations for this stage.

skip_if_unchanged.description: If true and DELTA mode is active, skip this stage when no inputs have changed since the previous run.
